vocabulary_principles: - Grouping: Distribute words evenly across Groups A, B, and C with a balanced mix of parts of speech.
- Content: Each entry must include POS, English definitions, and Chinese translations.
- Authenticity: Examples and translations must strictly match the original textbook sentences.
- Word Families: Split derivatives into individual entries with full details and examples.
story_principles: - Difficulty: Align stories with CEFR levels (A2, B1, B2, C1), with C2 for the Group Bonus.
- Variety: Employ diverse genres (e.g., emails, news, blogs, scripts, or commentaries).
- Clues: Provide sufficient context clues (collocations, synonyms) for logical deduction.
- Bonus Story: Must cover all words in the unit and maintain a professional/academic tone.
- Scrambling: Ensure the answer sequence for every story in each group is randomized to enhance challenge.
format_standards: - Highlighting: Mark target vocabulary using **word** bold syntax.
- Efficiency: Minify objects into single lines to reduce total file length for large datasets.
- Consistency: Maintain the hierarchy (vocabulary > Lesson > Group > words/stories).
language_principles: - All Chinese translations for example sentences and word definitions must exclusively use Traditional Chinese (Taiwan).

high_achiever.name: 高分好手

effortful_learner.description: 在任何測驗中獲得60分以上。

a2_explorer.description: 完成一個單元內所有組別的一顆星難度測驗（80分以上）。

b1_voyager.name: 旅行家
b1_voyager.description: 完成一個單元內所有組別的兩顆星難度測驗（80分以上）。

conqueror.name: 征服者
conqueror.description: 完成一個單元內所有組別的三顆星難度測驗(80分以上)。

strong_one.description: 完成一個單元內所有組別的四顆星難度測驗(80分以上)。

alien.description: 在一個單元的隱藏挑戰(❓)中獲得滿分。

cute_cat.name: 可愛貓貓
cute_cat.description: 是的，你沒看錯，宇宙的盡頭是貓貓。恭喜你完成所有挑戰，這隻可愛的橘貓是你的了。

stories.text: Yesterday, my friend Leo had a bad **injury** during the race. The **medical** team helped him before he could **collapse** from the pain. He didn't win a **medal** this time. However, because of his hard work and **dedication**, he will always be a **champion** to us.

Local runner Sarah Miller was the favorite to become the race **champion** this morning. However, halfway through, an old wall began to **collapse** near the track due to a minor earthquake. Sarah stopped running to help an elderly spectator who had suffered a head **injury** from falling debris. She stayed until the **medical** staff arrived. Even though she finished last, the committee awarded her a special **medal** for her bravery. They praised her **dedication** to the community.

What truly defines a professional athlete? Is it the shiny gold **medal** hanging on the wall, or the title of world **champion**? While these are symbols of success, they are merely the results of years of invisible **dedication**. Athletes often push their bodies to the absolute limit, sometimes to the point where their physical systems **collapse** under the pressure. When a career-threatening **injury** occurs, it is not just a physical challenge. Beyond the reach of modern **medical** science, it is the athlete’s inner will that determines if they can return.

Behind the roaring cheers lies a grueling reality. To pursue an Olympic **medal**, one must surrender everything. This level of **dedication** is often romanticized, yet it borders on obsession. We witness elite sprinters whose world-class forms suddenly **collapse** in the final meters, victims of immense physiological strain. Often, a single **injury** is all it takes to derail a decade of preparation, rendering one a former **champion** overnight. While the **medical** interventions available today are sophisticated, they cannot always mend a shattered dream.

My piano concert was a big **success** last night. Before I went on stage, I was so nervous and felt a little **pain** in my stomach. However, I saw you in the crowd and felt your **support**. It gave me the **courage** to play my best. The music was very **emotional**, and some people even cried. After the show, the theater **staff** told me I did a great job.

Nurses often see patients in great physical **pain**, which can be very **emotional** for everyone involved. To keep going, the hospital **staff** need a lot of **courage** and mental strength to face life and death. They also rely on the **support** of their colleagues to get through long, tiring shifts. When a patient finally recovers and goes home, it is considered a wonderful **success** for the entire team.

Many legendary leaders have experienced the **pain** of losing everything before reaching the top. What keeps them moving is often the **emotional** connection they have with their mission. It takes true **courage** to admit when a plan has failed and to try again. Moreover, the **support** from a dedicated team is crucial, as no one can achieve greatness alone. Ultimately, even a small group of **staff** members can change the world. Their grit is the real secret to long-term **success**.

Resilience requires a highly trained **staff** capable of maintaining focus during extreme volatility. When an industry faces a downturn, the psychological **pain** experienced by employees can lead to burnout. Therefore, providing robust **support** systems is a strategic necessity. Leaders must exhibit the professional **courage** to make unpopular decisions for long-term stability. Such high-pressure environments are inevitably **emotional**, yet they serve as the ultimate litmus test for institutional **success**.

Yesterday, Sam saw a cat stuck in a tall tree. The cat began to **struggle** to get down. Sam knew he had to help **immediately**, so he started to climb. He needed all his **strength** to reach the branch. When he finally brought the cat down safely, all the neighbors looked at him with **admiration**. Sam’s parents felt great **pride** in their son. Now, everyone in the street gives Sam a lot of **respect**.

From the start, the steep path was a constant **struggle** for my tired legs. I soon realized that I had underestimated the physical **strength** required for this altitude. When a thick fog rolled in, our guide told us to stop **immediately**. As I looked up at the snowy peak, I felt a deep sense of **admiration** for the professional climbers. They certainly deserve our **respect**. Although I didn't reach the top, I still take **pride** in how high I climbed.

Eleanor Vance dedicated her life to the **struggle** for social justice. Whenever she witnessed unfairness, she would react **immediately** by organizing a protest. Her unwavering moral **strength** allowed her to face powerful opponents without fear. Over the decades, she earned the **respect** of world leaders and citizens alike. Today, visitors express their **admiration** for her sacrifices. Her story remains a source of national **pride**.

In moments of crisis, the ego must be set aside so that one can respond **immediately** to the dictates of conscience. This requires a rare form of inner **strength** that is cultivated through years of reflection. We do not grant our **respect** to those who simply succeed, but to those who maintain their integrity while experiencing a deep **struggle**. Such resilience evokes a profound **admiration** that transcends boundaries. Ultimately, a healthy sense of **pride** is born from the knowledge that one has remained upright.

story.text: The pursuit of athletic excellence is a grueling path paved with paradoxical trials. To eventually emerge as a world **champion**, one must possess a level of **dedication** that borders on the ascetic, sacrificing years for the singular hope of an Olympic **medal**. Yet, a sudden, debilitating **injury** can render a lifetime of preparation moot. When the physical framework begins to **collapse** under immense physiological pressure, the presence of an elite **medical** team is indispensable. The **emotional** toll of a failed campaign is often far more corrosive than the visceral **pain** of a torn ligament. In these dark corridors of defeat, the unwavering **support** of a loyal coaching **staff** becomes the only tether to one's former identity. It requires a rare species of **courage** to find the inner **strength** to rise from the ashes. The ensuing **struggle** to reclaim one's status is a psychological quest to regain self-**respect**. One must act **immediately** to prevent cynicism from taking root, replacing despair with a quiet **pride** in the resilience of the soul. Those who navigate this journey evoke a profound **admiration** from the global community, proving that true **success** is an enduring testament to the indomitable heart.

stories.text: Our principal made an **announcement** this morning about the school cafeteria. He said that some students did **research** to find out what we like to eat. **normally**, we eat chicken or pork, but many students want more **flavor** in their meals. In **comparison** with last year, the food is now much healthier. However, the cooks say it is sometimes hard to **distinguish** between the different spices. We must read the salt amounts **accurately** to make the food taste better.

I recently read an official **announcement** about a new smart kitchen scale. According to the company's **research**, most home cooks fail to measure spices **accurately**. In **comparison** with traditional scales, this one can **distinguish** between over 500 different types of powder. While I **normally** just guess the amounts, this device helps me add the perfect **flavor** to every dish I make.

New **research** into the psychology of taste suggests that our environment affects us more than we realize. Scientists **normally** focus on the tongue, but a recent **announcement** highlights the role of sound. In **comparison** with quiet rooms, noisy places make it harder for people to **distinguish** subtle notes in their food. To **accurately** measure this effect, volunteers ate the same meal in different settings. They found that a loud background can significantly reduce the perceived **flavor** of a dessert.

Today's lecture focuses on the sensory **comparison** between virtual and physical reality. While we **normally** perceive the world through a blend of five senses, digital interfaces often fail to **distinguish** the nuances of smell and touch. Recent **research** has led to an **announcement** of a new 'e-tongue' technology. This device can **accurately** simulate any **flavor** by using controlled electrical pulses.

Last week, I was a **passenger** on a big bus to the mountains. The driver told us that the high altitude might **affect** our breathing. He said that if we feel **pressure** in our ears, we should swallow some water. It is important not to let the loud music **distract** us from the beautiful view outside. Some signs on the bus **indicate** where the emergency exits are. We should not **blame** the driver if the bus moves slowly on the steep hills, as the engine must **function** properly.

Did you know that stress can **affect** your body in many ways? When you are under a lot of **pressure**, your brain might not **function** as well as it usually does. Some studies **indicate** that constant noise can **distract** you from deep sleep. People often **blame** their coffee for their headaches. As a frequent **passenger** on long-distance flights, I have learned that staying hydrated is key.

In modern offices, the constant flow of emails can easily **distract** employees from their core tasks. Many workers **blame** technology for their lack of focus, but the problem is often deeper. Research results **indicate** that high levels of social **pressure** can negatively **affect** mental health. If a team is to **function** effectively, leaders must ensure that every **passenger** on the corporate journey feels valued.

The efficiency of public transit systems can significantly **affect** the economic growth of a city. When a subway line fails to **function** during peak hours, every **passenger** suffers from lost time. Data from recent surveys **indicate** that overcrowding can **distract** commuters and increase their stress levels. City officials often **blame** outdated infrastructure, but the intense **pressure** from rapid population growth is the primary driver.

I tried a new **recipe** for chocolate cake yesterday. The most important **ingredient** is dark chocolate. My mom had to **warn** me not to use too much sugar. I hope the kitchen can **remain** clean while I work! To **provide** a sweet taste, I added some honey. The baking **conditions** were perfect, and the smell was very **pleasant**. My family said this cake was the best **solution** for our hungry stomachs!

Finding a **pleasant** place to stay during your holiday is crucial. I recommend the Sunset Hotel because they **provide** excellent service to all guests. The hotel staff will **warn** you about the weather **conditions** before you go hiking. If you are looking for a **solution** to your stress, their spa is the perfect choice. You should also try their famous seafood soup; the secret **ingredient** is fresh ginger. Even after many years, the hotel's high quality has managed to **remain** the same.

To protect our oceans, we must find a long-term **solution** to plastic pollution. Scientists **warn** that if current **conditions** do not change, there will be more plastic than fish by 2050. Many companies now **provide** eco-friendly packaging as a better alternative. However, for these efforts to **remain** effective, we need global cooperation. Every single **ingredient** in our industrial products should be evaluated. Following a 'green **recipe**' for manufacturing is a necessity for a **pleasant** future.

In the competitive hospitality industry, the ability to **provide** a consistent and **pleasant** customer experience is paramount. When economic **conditions** fluctuate, businesses must find a creative **solution** to maintain their market share. Experts **warn** against cutting costs by reducing the quality of a core **ingredient** in the service model. Instead, companies should ensure that their values **remain** central to their operations. A successful business **recipe** involves a delicate balance.

story.text: The aviation industry’s recent **announcement** regarding enhanced dining protocols has sparked a fascinating **comparison** between tradition and innovation. Extensive **research** into high-altitude physiology continues to **indicate** that an airplane cabin is an environment where our biological systems do not **function** as they **normally** do. For a start, the low humidity and **pressure** significantly **affect** our olfactory senses, making it difficult to **distinguish** between subtle notes in a complex dish. Consequently, food that would be considered delicious on the ground can often **remain** disappointingly bland in the sky. To **accurately** address this, airlines must **provide** a culinary **solution** that accounts for these unique **conditions**. Chefs often **warn** that a standard **recipe** must be modified, frequently adding more seasoning or a savory **ingredient** like tomato or mushroom to boost the **flavor**. However, even the most meticulously prepared cuisine can fail if a **passenger** is constantly unsettled. The persistent noise of the engines can **distract** the mind, further numbing the tongue’s sensitivity to sweetness and salt. We cannot simply **blame** the catering staff for a mediocre meal when the very laws of physics are working against them. Ultimately, achieving a **pleasant** dining experience at thirty thousand feet is a triumph of both science and art.

stories.text: Yesterday was very cold. I saw a little dog in the street and it began to **shiver**. I tried **desperately** to find its owner. Finally, a girl came and started to **explain** that the dog had run away. I **reluctantly** said goodbye to the dog. The girl walked away with such **grace** that everyone looked at her. I almost felt like I would **faint** from excitement! This was a great lesson, even if it wasn't a **scientific** experiment.

During my trip to the mountains, the temperature dropped and I started to **shiver** in my thin jacket. I **reluctantly** decided to call for help. My guide later had to **explain** that mountain weather changes quickly. I was searching **desperately** for my camp when I saw a light. The way the stars shone was full of natural **grace**. I was so tired that I thought I might **faint**, but I made it back. This trip proved that nature doesn't follow a simple **scientific** rule.

A rigorous **scientific** approach is often required to study emotions. For instance, researchers try to **explain** why we react physically to fear. When a person is in danger, they might **shiver** or even **faint** as the body shuts down. In one study, participants **reluctantly** entered a dark room to test their limits. They worked **desperately** to solve puzzles under pressure. Some individuals maintain a surprising level of **grace** and composure even in stress.

Hawthorne's narrative often pits the cold, **scientific** mind against the fluid **grace** of the human spirit. In the story, the husband cannot **explain** his obsession with perfection. He **reluctantly** acknowledges his wife's beauty but **desperately** seeks to alter it. We see the wife **shiver** at the thought of his experiments, eventually reaching a point where she might **faint** from the psychological strain. Imperfection is often more beautiful than logic.

I had a hard day. My math teacher asked me to **describe** a triangle, but I forgot. I felt a lot of **frustration** because I studied all night. I could **hardly** keep my eyes open. Then, my friend got into a **rage** because he lost his pen. I told him he was being **stubborn** and should just buy a new one. I had to **confess** that I moved it by mistake. I told him with great **sincerity**, and he finally smiled.

Witnesses **describe** a local man being in a total **rage** after his dog was hit by a bicycle. He was so **stubborn** that he refused to leave until the police arrived. Later, he had to **confess** that he wasn't paying attention to his pet. The cyclist expressed his **sincerity** by offering to help. Many people feel a sense of **frustration** because the park paths are too narrow. You can **hardly** walk there without bumping into someone.

Effective communication is the key to avoiding **frustration** in the workplace. When a project fails, do not fly into a **rage** and blame your team. Instead, clearly **describe** what went wrong. If you are too **stubborn** to change your strategy, you will **hardly** see any improvement. It is better to **confess** your mistakes early on. If you speak with true **sincerity**, your colleagues will respect you more and work harder.

The phenomenon of being **stubborn** in academic circles often stems from a fear of failure. Scholars who **hardly** ever admit to being wrong experience intense **frustration** when challenged. In professional circles, it is rare for an expert to **confess** a lack of knowledge. However, the **sincerity** of a researcher is their most valuable asset. Those who can calmly **describe** their limits rather than falling into a **rage** are more likely to make breakthroughs.

I found an old toy in the garden. It was very dirty, and I could see no **trace** of its original color. My mom told me that the color would **fade** if I left it in the sun. I felt some **regret**. I tried to **restore** it by cleaning it. It took a lot of **concentration** to fix the small parts. After I finished, I saw my friend **depart** from his house to go to the park, and I showed him the toy.

Although the wall paintings started to **fade** long ago, you can still see a **trace** of the beautiful gold leaf. The government plans to **restore** the building next year. This task will require great **concentration** from the artists. Many visitors feel a bit of **regret** when they see the broken stones. They often watch the sun set before they **depart**, thinking about the long history of this special place.

Conservationists are working hard to **restore** local wetlands. Many rare plants have begun to **fade** away due to pollution. Today, there is almost no **trace** of the birds that used to live here. The project requires intense **concentration** on soil quality. If we do not act now, we will deeply **regret** our choices. As the migratory birds **depart**, we are reminded of how fragile our ecosystem really is.

The attempt to **restore** a masterpiece is a balance between science and intuition. Conservators must ensure that original brushstrokes do not **fade**. Even a tiny **trace** of the wrong chemical can ruin a painting. This process demands absolute **concentration**, as there is no room for **regret**. As we see the older generation of artists **depart**, we must ask ourselves how much of their soul remains in the works.

story.text: In Hawthorne's 'The Birthmark,' we confront the destructive nature of the human ego. Aylmer, a man of formidable **scientific** intellect, finds himself unable to **explain** his visceral disgust toward his wife’s minor blemish. He **reluctantly** admits that Georgiana is a vision of **grace**, yet he **desperately** yearns to **restore** her to absolute perfection. As Georgiana watches her husband fly into a **rage** over his failed formulas, she begins to **shiver** with a premonition. She could **hardly** have imagined that his **stubborn** **concentration** on her flaw would eventually make her **depart** from this world. Aylmer attempts to **describe** the **sincerity** of his love, but he fails to realize that his **frustration** with nature forces him to **confess** his own limits. As the birthmark begins to **fade**, so does the light in Georgiana’s eyes. Watching her **faint** into the arms of death, Aylmer is left with nothing but a lingering **trace** of her presence and a weight of **regret**. He realized too late that true beauty must be loved before it is forced to **depart**.

stories.text: In our **region**, the **principal** of the school said the school rules **forbid** us to use phones in class. However, many **female** students choose to **continue** to bring them. They want to get a good **education** so they can have **equal** chances to find a job in the future.

The government announced that **female** **education** is now a **principal** concern. In this **region**, many communities used to **forbid** girls from attending school. Now, they **continue** to build new facilities to ensure **equal** opportunities for all children.

To achieve true social equality, we must **continue** to invest in human capital. In certain **regions**, cultural norms still **forbid** women from seeking higher **education**. The **principal** responsibility of any leader is to ensure that **female** voices are recognized as **equal** partners in progress.

The systemic barriers that **forbid** access to **education** based on gender remain a **principal** challenge in developing **regions**. Advocates **continue** to push for **equal** treatment, noting that **female** empowerment is the cornerstone of regional economic growth and stability.

My brother loves to **annoy** me by being too loud. I told him he needs to **control** his behavior. Last month, he had **surgery** on his leg and had to **sign** many papers at the hospital. Now he needs to **recover** at home. I tried to **persuade** him to rest, but he started to **threaten** to go out and play!

After the car accident, the victim had to undergo emergency **surgery**. Doctors told him he would **recover** if he stayed under their **control**. However, his boss tried to **threaten** to fire him if he didn't **sign** the new contract immediately. I had to **persuade** him that his health was more important than letting a bad job **annoy** him.

Modern hospitals utilize advanced robotics to **control** the environment during complex **surgery**. The long recovery period will often **annoy** patients. Surgeons must **persuade** them that following a strict plan is the only way to prevent bacteria from starting to **threaten** their health. Before any treatment, the patient must **sign** a legal consent form.

Psychological warfare often involves the use of subtle ways to **threaten** citizens to **control** public perception. Leaders attempt to **persuade** the masses by highlighting how external forces might **annoy** them through social inconveniences. In times of crisis, the state may need to **sign** international treaties to ensure a quick economic ability to **recover**, acting with the precision of a surgeon during **surgery**.

Our school decided to **adopt** a new **policy**. We want to **honor** the best student every month. This will **ensure** that everyone works hard. We must remember that a pen is a better **weapon** than a sword. We will **establish** a small library to show our history.

The local council plans to **establish** a new law to **ensure** public safety. They will **adopt** a strict **policy** against carrying any dangerous **weapon**. To **honor** those who protect us, there will be a ceremony. The establishment of this rule is a great step for our neighborhood.

International organizations are trying to persuade nations to **adopt** a peaceful **policy**. They want to **establish** a global fund to **ensure** that children are safe from the effects of any harmful **weapon**. It is a great **honor** to work for such a cause. The establishment of peace is a goal for all humanity.

The need to **establish** a robust legal **policy** is vital to **ensure** long-term national security. Nations must **adopt** stringent measures to control the flow of any **weapon**. We should **honor** those who seek diplomatic solutions instead of violence. Truly, knowledge is the most formidable tool in the modern world.

story.text: The saga of Malala Yousafzai serves as a profound indictment of any regime that seeks to **control** the human mind through violence. In a **region** where the **principal** power belonged to the Taliban, the regime would strictly **forbid** young **female** students from pursuing an **education**. Despite attempts to **threaten** her for her activism, Malala chose to **continue** to advocate for **equal** rights, a stance that would deeply **annoy** the ruling extremists. Following a near-fatal attack that necessitated extensive **surgery**, she managed to **recover**, eventually using her global platform to **persuade** the world to **sign** a petition for change. This international outcry compelled the government to **adopt** a progressive **policy** to **ensure** educational access. Her work to **establish** a foundation has since served to **honor** her vision, proving that an enlightened mind is a more potent **weapon** against tyranny than any physical force.

stories.text: I love to **entertain** my friends. Yesterday, we went to see a street **parade**. The clowns were very **imaginative** with their costumes. I had a special **request** for a balloon animal, and they made it quickly. They **consistently** smiled at everyone. The music helped to **convey** a happy feeling. I didn't **dare** to touch the big drum, though!

Our drama club decided to **entertain** the school with a new play. The director had a very **imaginative** vision for the stage design. We **consistently** practiced every afternoon. The main goal was to **convey** a message about friendship. Before the show, the principal made a formal **request** that everyone turn off their phones. It felt like a grand **parade** when we walked onto the stage. I hardly **dare** look at the audience at first.

As a marketing manager, my job is to **consistently** find new ways to **entertain** our target audience. We recently launched a highly **imaginative** ad campaign. The visuals perfectly **convey** the core values of our brand. During the product launch, we organized a massive digital **parade** online. We also submitted a **request** to influencers to share our content. Some competitors wouldn't **dare** to try such an unconventional approach.

Authors of speculative fiction must employ highly **imaginative** scenarios to **entertain** their readers while exploring profound themes. They **consistently** use complex metaphors to **convey** underlying societal critiques. A formal **request** from the publisher often dictates the tone of the narrative. Unlike a superficial **parade** of events, a well-crafted plot requires depth. Few novice writers **dare** to challenge established literary conventions so overtly.

My brother has a true **passion** for music. He wants to **achieve** his dream of being a singer. It is a hard **career** to start. Sometimes, he faces **extreme** tiredness after working all day. He knows that many artists start in **poverty**. However, his friends always come to his **rescue** when he needs help. He can face any **hardship** because he loves singing.

To **achieve** success in the medical field, you need true **passion**. Doctor Lin chose this **career** to help people in need. He often travels to areas of **extreme** cold to treat patients. These regions suffer from both bad weather and widespread **poverty**. He has faced a lot of personal **hardship** during his travels. Last year, he even helped to **rescue** a family trapped in a snowstorm.

Building a successful political **career** often requires overcoming significant **hardship**. Leaders must show a genuine **passion** for public service to **achieve** meaningful change. For instance, tackling systemic **poverty** requires innovative policies rather than just temporary charity. During times of **extreme** economic crisis, a government must implement swift measures to **rescue** failing industries.

The pursuit of a distinguished academic **career** is fraught with intellectual **hardship**. Scholars must possess an unwavering **passion** to **achieve** groundbreaking discoveries. Throughout history, many scientific pioneers operated in relative **poverty**, driven solely by curiosity. They often took **extreme** measures to fund their research. Ultimately, their contributions served to **rescue** humanity from ignorance.

I like to **weave** baskets in my free time. It is a fun **challenge** for me. Sometimes I have a bad **attitude** when I make a mistake. But I try to **overcome** this feeling. My teacher says every mistake is an **obstacle** to learn from. Being a beginner is a **disadvantage**, but I practice every day. I want to **fulfill** my goal and **deliver** a perfect basket to my mom.

Our team had to **deliver** a big project by Friday. At first, our small budget was a major **disadvantage**. However, we decided to adopt a positive **attitude**. We managed to **weave** all our different ideas into one great plan. Every **obstacle** we faced became a new **challenge** to solve. Working together helped us **overcome** our fears and **fulfill** our boss's expectations.

Successful entrepreneurs must **deliver** high-quality products regardless of market conditions. A sudden lack of funding can put a startup at a severe **disadvantage**. Yet, a resilient **attitude** allows founders to view every **obstacle** as a learning opportunity. They must skillfully **weave** together various marketing strategies to **overcome** consumer doubts. Only by facing the core **challenge** directly can a company truly **fulfill** its corporate mission.

Orators of historical significance possess the rare ability to **weave** compelling narratives that **deliver** profound emotional impact. They recognize that an entrenched societal **attitude** can act as a formidable **obstacle** to progress. Operating from a political **disadvantage** often amplifies the **challenge** of reform. To **overcome** such systemic resistance, leaders must articulate a vision that can **fulfill** the aspirations of an entire generation.

story.text: In the highly competitive arena of global storytelling, creators must **consistently** push boundaries to **entertain** increasingly discerning audiences. Walt Disney’s **career** exemplifies this ethos, born from an authentic **passion** to **convey** hope. He did not merely present a superficial **parade** of animations; he utilized highly **imaginative** narratives to **deliver** profound emotional resonance. Early on, Disney faced the severe **disadvantage** of financial ruin and the stark reality of **poverty**. Yet, he maintained a resolute **attitude**, refusing to let **extreme** fiscal **hardship** act as a permanent **obstacle**. To **fulfill** his ambitious vision, he had to **overcome** relentless skepticism and the daunting **challenge** of technological limitations. He would boldly **request** unparalleled dedication from his team, leading a collective effort to **rescue** his studio from the brink of collapse. Few would **dare** to **weave** such ambitious dreams into reality, but it was precisely this tenacity that allowed him to **achieve** a legacy that continues to inspire millions.

champion.definition: the winner of a sports event or a competition
champion.example: Howard will be crowned the world chess champion after winning the final next Monday.
champion.example_tw: 在贏得下週一的決賽後，Howard 將被加冕為世界西洋棋冠軍。

dedication.definition: time and effort one puts into achieving a goal
dedication.example: Doctor Parker shows total dedication to this project. She has spent a lot of time doing detailed studies.
dedication.example_tw: Parker 醫生對這個計畫展現了全然的投入。她花了許多時間進行詳盡的研究。

dedicate.definition: to give time and effort to a particular purpose
dedicate.example: For eighteen months, this author has dedicated himself to writing a book about night markets.
dedicate.example_tw: 十八個月來，這位作者一直致力於撰寫一本關於夜市的書。

medal.definition: piece of metal awarded to someone who has won a race
medal.example: Liz dreamed of coming in first in the race and wearing a shiny gold medal around her neck.
medal.example_tw: Liz 夢想著在比賽中獲得第一名，並在脖子上戴著一面閃亮的金牌。

injury.definition: physical harm done to one's body
injury.example: After suffering a serious injury in her last race, the cyclist has no chance of winning this time.
injury.example_tw: 在上一次比賽中受了重傷後，這位自行車手這次沒有機會獲勝了。

injure.definition: to cause physical harm to someone or something
injure.example: He injured his knee while playing basketball.
injure.example_tw: 他在打籃球時傷了膝蓋。

success.definition: the fact that someone achieves something very well
success.example: Monica had great success in her work and became a famous singer after years of dedication.
success.example_tw: Monica 在工作上取得了巨大的成功，並在多年的奉獻後成為了一名著名的歌手。

succeed.definition: to achieve the desired aim or result
succeed.example: If you work hard, you will eventually succeed.
succeed.example_tw: 如果你努力工作，你最終會成功的。

successful.definition: achieving the results wanted or hoped for
successful.example: The company was highly successful in the European market.
successful.example_tw: 這家公司在歐洲市場非常成功。

pain.definition: what you feel when you are hurt or sick
pain.example: Alex experienced a sharp pain in his leg while hiking.
pain.example_tw: Alex 在健行時感覺到腿部劇痛。

painful.example: It is painful to watch the animals suffer.
painful.example_tw: 看著動物們受苦是令人痛苦的。

painfully.definition: in a way that causes pain
painfully.example: His leg was painfully twisted in the accident.
painfully.example_tw: 在車禍中，他的腿被痛苦地扭傷了。

immediately.example: I need to leave immediately, or I'll miss my plane.
immediately.example_tw: 我需要立刻離開，否則我會趕不上飛機。

immediate.example: The manager asked for an immediate response to the problem.
immediate.example_tw: 經理要求對這個問題做出立即的回應。

collapse.definition: to fall down, especially when one faints
collapse.example: The woman felt dizzy after the seven-hour journey and suddenly collapsed in the station.
collapse.example_tw: 這名女子在七小時的旅程後感到暈眩，並在車站突然倒下。

medical.definition: related to the treatment of injury or disease
medical.example: Lisa plans to attend medical school and become a doctor in the future.
medical.example_tw: Lisa 計畫就讀醫學院，未來成為一名醫生。

staff.definition: a group or team of workers or employees
staff.example: The hotel staff greets all guests in a polite way.
staff.example_tw: 飯店員工以禮貌的方式迎接所有客人。

courage.definition: ability to face a difficult or dangerous situation
courage.example: It takes courage for first-time swimmers to put their heads under the water.
courage.example_tw: 對於第一次游泳的人來說，把頭伸進水裡是需要勇氣的。

strength.example: It's important for a player to have enough mental strength to handle the stress.
strength.example_tw: 對於一名選手來說，擁有足夠的心理韌性來應對壓力是很重要的。

strengthen.definition: to make something stronger or more effective
strengthen.example: Exercise helps to strengthen your heart and lungs.
strengthen.example_tw: 運動有助於強化你的心肺功能。

support.example: The crowd cheered loudly to show their support for the tennis player.
support.example_tw: 群眾大聲歡呼以表示對網球選手的支持。

struggle.definition: to move with great difficulty; a hard fight
struggle.example: With a heavy load in back, the truck struggled slowly up the steep hill.
struggle.example_tw: 由於後方載貨沉重，卡車艱難地緩慢爬上陡峭的山丘。

pride.example: Neil took pride in his brother's determination when he saw him complete the marathon.
pride.example_tw: 當 Neil 看到哥哥完成馬拉松時，他為哥哥的決心感到自豪。

proud.definition: feeling deep pleasure or satisfaction as a result of one's own achievements
proud.example: Her parents are very proud of her excellent grades.
proud.example_tw: 她的父母為她優異的成績感到非常驕傲。

emotional.example: Mr. Jones got very emotional and struggled to hold back the tears during the graduation.
emotional.example_tw: 畢業典禮上，瓊斯先生情緒非常激動，努力忍住眼淚。

emotion.definition: a strong feeling such as love, fear, or anger
emotion.example: Music can express a wide range of human emotions.
emotion.example_tw: 音樂可以表達廣泛的人類情感。

admiration.definition: feeling of deep approval for someone
admiration.example: Fans showed their admiration for the amazing actor by throwing flowers onto the stage.
admiration.example_tw: 影迷們將花扔上舞台，以表達對這位了不起的演員的欽佩。

respect.definition: feeling of admiration for one's qualities
respect.example: Mike had great respect for the wise old man and always treated him politely.
respect.example_tw: Mike 非常尊敬這位睿智的老人，總是很有禮貌地對待他。

warn.definition: to tell someone about possible danger
warn.example: Bill's parents warned him not to go mountain climbing because a typhoon was coming.
warn.example_tw: Bill 的父母警告他不要去爬山，因為颱風快來了。

warning.definition: an official statement that tells people about a possible danger
warning.example: The moment the fire department issued a warning that the building might collapse, everyone inside got out immediately.
warning.example_tw: 消防部門一發布大樓可能倒塌的警告，裡面的每個人都立即撤離了。

announcement.definition: official statement to inform people of an event
announcement.example: Our English teacher made an announcement that there would be a test on Thursday.
announcement.example_tw: 我們的英文老師宣布週四要考試。

announce.definition: to make a public or official statement
announce.example: This world champion boxer has announced that he will retire at the end of March.
announce.example_tw: 這位世界冠軍拳擊手已宣布他將於三月底退休。

research.definition: detailed study of a particular issue
research.example: Dr. Smith is doing some research into how dolphins communicate with each other.
research.example_tw: Smith 博士正在研究海豚如何互相溝通。

flavor.example: The soup has no flavor. You should add some salt to it.
flavor.example_tw: 這湯沒味道。你應該加點鹽。

pressure.definition: force produced by a liquid or gas; mental stress
pressure.example: Doctors often warn patients with high blood pressure to avoid high-fat diets.
pressure.example_tw: 醫生常警告高血壓患者避免高脂肪飲食。

normally.example: Uncle Frank normally reads the newspaper over breakfast.
normally.example_tw: Frank 叔叔通常在吃早餐時讀報。

normal.example: It is normal to feel nervous before a big exam.
normal.example_tw: 在大考前感到緊張是正常的。

distinguish.definition: to identify things or people as being different
distinguish.example: Nancy and her twin sister look so similar that even their parents can't distinguish them.
distinguish.example_tw: Nancy 和她的雙胞胎妹妹長得太像了，有時連父母都無法分辨。

accurately.example: So far scientists have not found a way to predict earthquakes accurately.
accurately.example_tw: 到目前為止，科學家還沒有找到準確預測地震的方法。

accurate.definition: correct, exact, and without any mistakes
accurate.example: The victim provided an accurate description of the thief.
accurate.example_tw: 受害者提供了對小偷的精確描述。

function.definition: to operate in a useful way; purpose
function.example: Malcolm's favorite couch also functions as a bed for guests.
function.example_tw: 當有客人留宿時，Malcolm 最喜歡的沙發也可以充當床。

blame.definition: to see someone or something as the reason for a problem
blame.example: The police blamed the drunken driver for the terrible car accident.
blame.example_tw: 警方將這起可怕的車禍歸咎於醉酒司機。

affect.definition: to cause someone or something to change
affect.example: Global warming has greatly affected the lives of many wild animals.
affect.example_tw: 全球暖化大大影響了許多野生動物的生存。

passenger.definition: someone in a vehicle, except for the driver
passenger.example: Passengers on the plane were glad that strong winds wouldn't affect their flight.
passenger.example_tw: 飛機上的乘客很高興強風不會影響他們的航程。

distract.definition: to take someone's attention away from their task
distract.example: Loud music from next door kept distracting Ken from his reading.
distract.example_tw: 隔壁巨大的音樂聲一直讓 Ken 無法專心閱讀。

distraction.definition: something that prevents someone from giving full attention to something else
distraction.example: I can't study in the living room because there are too many distractions.
distraction.example_tw: 我無法在客廳讀書，因為有太多令人分心的事物。

indicate.example: Recent research clearly indicates that only 3% of children get enough exercise.
indicate.example_tw: 最近的研究清楚顯示，只有 3% 的兒童得到了應有的運動量。

indication.definition: a sign that something exists, is true, or is likely to happen
indication.example: Dark clouds are an indication that it might rain soon.
indication.example_tw: 烏雲是即將下雨的跡象。

conditions.definition: situation or environment that affects something
conditions.example: Under normal conditions, my scooter functions well.
conditions.example_tw: 在正常情況下，我的機車運作良好。

condition.definition: the state that something or someone is in
condition.example: The used car is still in excellent condition.
condition.example_tw: 這輛二手車的狀況依然極佳。

solution.definition: a way to solve a problem
solution.example: The government is trying to think of a solution to air pollution.
solution.example_tw: 政府正試著想出一個解決空氣污染的方法。

solve.definition: to find an answer to a problem
solve.example: The detective worked day and night to solve the mystery.
solve.example_tw: 偵探日以繼夜地工作以解開這個謎團。

ingredient.definition: foods used to prepare a certain dish
ingredient.example: This restaurant only uses natural ingredients in its dishes.
ingredient.example_tw: 這家餐廳的菜餚只使用天然食材。

recipe.definition: list of ingredients and instructions for a dish
recipe.example: Does this cookbook include a recipe for apple pie?
recipe.example_tw: 這本食譜有包含蘋果派的作法嗎？

comparison.definition: checking how two or more things are similar or different
comparison.example: By comparison, it doesn't cost so much to live in other cities in Taiwan.
comparison.example_tw: 相較之下，在台灣其他城市生活的費用沒那麼高。

compare.definition: to examine the differences and similarities between two or more things
compare.example: It is not fair to compare the two students because they have different backgrounds.
compare.example_tw: 比較這兩名學生是不公平的，因為他們有著不同的背景。

remain.definition: to continue to be in the same condition
remain.example: Judy found that most things remained unchanged when she went back home.
remain.example_tw: 當 Judy 回到家鄉時，她發現大部分事物依然沒變。

provide.definition: to give someone something that he or she needs
provide.example: This hotel provides a nice breakfast and free Wi-Fi for its guests.
provide.example_tw: 這間飯店為客人提供美味早餐和免費無線網路。

pleasant.definition: enjoyable and making one feel happy
pleasant.example: It is pleasant to go for a walk in the evening when it is cooler.
pleasant.example_tw: 傍晚天氣較涼爽時去散步是很愜意的。

grace.definition: being attractive and in control during movement
grace.example: The cat leapt onto the windowsill with grace.
grace.example_tw: 那隻貓優雅地跳上窗臺。

graceful.definition: moving in a smooth and attractive way
graceful.example: The ballet dancer's movements were so graceful that everyone watched in silent admiration.
graceful.example_tw: 芭蕾舞者的動作如此優美，每個人都投以欽佩的目光。

desperately.definition: showing little hope and ready to try anything
desperately.example: The mother desperately searched the crowded mall for her missing child.
desperately.example_tw: 那位母親在擁擠的購物中心裡拚命尋找她失蹤的孩子。

desperate.definition: needing or wanting something very much
desperate.example: The little girl was desperate for her parents' attention, so she kept dropping spoons.
desperate.example_tw: 那個小女孩非常渴望父母的關注，所以她一直把湯匙和叉子掉到地板上。

reluctantly.definition: slowly because you do not want to do it
reluctantly.example: John reluctantly agreed to tidy his bedroom after being asked many times.
reluctantly.example_tw: 在被要求許多次後，約翰才不情願地同意整理臥室。

reluctant.example: Nora was having such a good time during her vacation that she was reluctant to leave.
reluctant.example_tw: 諾拉在度假時玩得很開心，以至於她不情願離開。

describe.definition: to give details about someone or something
describe.example: People often describe Zoe as someone who is dedicated to her work.
describe.example_tw: 人們常將柔伊描述為一個對工作投入的人。

description.definition: a statement that tells you how something or someone looks, sounds, etc.
description.example: The police asked the witness to give a detailed description of the suspect.
description.example_tw: 警方要求目擊者給出嫌犯的詳細描述。

shiver.definition: to suddenly shake because of cold or fear
shiver.example: Jamie shivered with fear as the large dog drew nearer.
shiver.example_tw: 隨著大狗靠近，潔米恐懼地顫抖著。

faint.definition: to become unconscious for a short time
faint.example: After Ben ran for an hour in the heat, he nearly fainted.
faint.example_tw: 班在酷熱中跑了一小時後，筋疲力盡，差點暈倒。

scientific.example: The students conducted a scientific experiment on plant growth.
scientific.example_tw: 學生們進行了一項關於植物生長的科學實驗。

frustration.definition: feeling of being annoyed or upset
frustration.example: Harry yelled out in frustration when he realized he was locked out.
frustration.example_tw: 哈利意識到自己被鎖在房門外時，懊惱地大叫了起來。

frustrate.definition: to make someone feel annoyed or less confident
frustrate.example: It frustrates me that I'm not able to communicate fluently in the new language.
frustrate.example_tw: 我無法流利地使用正在學習的新語言進行交流，這讓我很沮喪。

stubborn.definition: not wanting to change one's mind
stubborn.example: Despite his friends' advice, Jack remained stubborn.
stubborn.example_tw: 儘管朋友們提出了建議，傑克依然固執己見。

rage.definition: strong feeling of anger difficult to control
rage.example: Kris stormed out of the room in a rage after an argument.
rage.example_tw: 克里斯在爭吵後，憤怒地衝出了房間。

concentration.definition: ability to direct all effort toward a task
concentration.example: Julia began to lose concentration after studying for several hours.
concentration.example_tw: 茱莉亞在學習了幾個小時後開始無法集中注意力。

concentrate.definition: to give all your attention to something
concentrate.example: Sandy was concentrating on the video game so fully that she forgot her dinner.
concentrate.example_tw: 珊蒂如此專注於電子遊戲，以至於忘了吃晚飯。

explain.definition: to give a reason for something
explain.example: The coach carefully explained the rules of the competition.
explain.example_tw: 教練仔細地解釋了比賽規則。

explanation.definition: the details or reasons that someone gives to make something clear
explanation.example: The manager asked Lily to provide an explanation for her absence.
explanation.example_tw: 經理要求莉莉對上週缺席會議做出解釋。

sincerity.definition: being honest and saying what you mean
sincerity.example: Because George said sorry with sincerity, Amy decided to forgive him.
sincerity.example_tw: 因為喬治真誠地道歉，艾咪決定原諒他。

sincere.example: Sarah expressed her sincere thanks to the volunteers.
sincere.example_tw: 莎拉向志願者表示由衷的感謝。

confess.definition: to tell the truth about something bad done
confess.example: Kevin confessed that he was the one who actually drove the stolen car.
confess.example_tw: 凱文承認實際開那輛贓車的人是他。

confession.definition: a statement admitting that one is guilty of a crime
confession.example: The police questioned Tom for hours before he finally made a confession.
confession.example_tw: 警察審問了湯姆幾個小時，他最後才供認不諱。

restore.definition: to return something to the original state
restore.example: After a long battle with illness, Lisa is now fully restored to health.
restore.example_tw: 在與疾病長期搏鬥後，麗莎現在已完全恢復健康。

fade.definition: to disappear or grow weaker over time
fade.example: Mark's dream of becoming a writer started to fade when his novel sold poorly.
fade.example_tw: 馬克成為作家的夢想在他的小說銷路不佳時開始逐漸破滅。

hardly.example: Noah could hardly believe it when he heard her beautiful voice.
hardly.example_tw: 當他聽到她優美的聲音時，他幾乎不敢相信。

trace.definition: a mark or sign that something happened
trace.example: The search team found no clues; she had vanished without a trace.
trace.example_tw: 搜救隊沒有發現線索；她消失得無影蹤。

depart.definition: to leave a place or pass away
depart.example: The soldier departed this world peacefully after years of service.
depart.example_tw: 這位戰士在服役多年後平靜地告別了人世。

departure.definition: the act of leaving a place
departure.example: Due to rough seas, the ship's departure from the harbor had to be delayed.
departure.example_tw: 由於海面波濤洶湧，該船從港口啟程的時間被迫延遲。

regret.definition: sadness over something done or failed to be done
regret.example: Henry expressed deep regret at not spending more time with his mother.
regret.example_tw: 亨利對沒有花更多時間陪伴母親表示遺憾。

female.definition: being or referring to a woman
female.example: Amelia Earhart was the first female pilot to fly solo across the Atlantic.
female.example_tw: 艾米莉亞·埃爾哈特是第一位獨自飛越大西洋的女飛行員。

region.definition: a large land area sharing similar features
region.example: The northern regions of the country are usually windy and snowy.
region.example_tw: 這個國家的北部地區在冬天通常多風且多雪。

regional.definition: relating to a particular region or district
regional.example: The regional government is planning to build a new hospital here.
regional.example_tw: 地方政府計畫在這裡建造一所新醫院。

forbid.definition: not to allow someone to do something
forbid.example: The government cleaned up the coast by forbidding factories to dump waste.
forbid.example_tw: 政府透過禁止工廠傾倒廢棄物來清理海岸。

continue.definition: to carry on, or not to stop doing something
continue.example: After a short break for lunch, Wally continued his work.
continue.example_tw: 午餐短暫休息後，沃利繼續他的工作。

continuous.example: The brain needs a continuous supply of blood to function properly.
continuous.example_tw: 大腦需要持續不斷的血液供應才能正常運作。

education.definition: system of teaching usually in a school
education.example: Two new schools were built to improve the quality of education.
education.example_tw: 新建了兩所學校以提高教育品質。

educational.example: This computer game is not only fun but also educational.
educational.example_tw: 這款電腦遊戲不僅有趣，而且具有教育意義。

educate.example: The organization aims to educate the public about environmental issues.
educate.example_tw: 該組織旨在向大眾宣導環境議題。

principal.definition: someone in charge of a school; primary
principal.example: Mrs. Lee has been the school's principal for ten years.
principal.example_tw: 李太太擔任這所學校的校長已有十年了。

equal.definition: having the same rights or opportunities
equal.example: Everyone should enjoy equal rights and be treated the same.
equal.example_tw: 每個人都應該享有平等的權利，並受到同樣的對待。

equality.example: The goal of the movement is to achieve racial equality.
equality.example_tw: 該運動的目標是實現種族平等。

control.definition: to limit or rule something or behavior
control.example: It is important to control your weight for your health.
control.example_tw: 為了健康，控制體重很重要。

annoy.definition: to cause someone to become angry
annoy.example: It really annoys Mrs. Donald when her children don't wash their hands.
annoy.example_tw: 當唐納德太太的孩子們不洗手時，她真的很生氣。

threaten.definition: warn someone that you will harm them
threaten.example: The robber threatened the bank staff with a gun.
threaten.example_tw: 搶匪用槍威脅銀行工作人員。

threat.definition: something that is likely to cause harm
threat.example: Global warming poses a serious threat to our environment.
threat.example_tw: 全球暖化對我們的環境構成了嚴重威脅。

surgery.definition: medical treatment in which a doctor cuts open a body
surgery.example: When Joel had a heart operation, a young doctor performed the surgery.
surgery.example_tw: 當喬爾接受心臟手術時，是一名年輕醫生動的手術。

recover.definition: to heal or get better after sickness
recover.example: Stacy recovered from the flu and went back to work last week.
recover.example_tw: 史黛西感冒痊癒後，上週回到了工作崗位。

recovery.definition: the process of becoming healthy again
recovery.example: The doctor says that a full recovery will take several months.
recovery.example_tw: 醫生說完全康復需要幾個月的時間。

sign.definition: to write one's name to show agreement
sign.example: David signed his name at the bottom of the letter.
sign.example_tw: 大衛在信的末尾簽了名。

persuade.definition: to use arguments to make someone do something
persuade.example: Mr. Kennedy tried to persuade his daughter to do more exercise.
persuade.example_tw: 甘迺迪先生試圖說服他的女兒多做運動。

persuasion.example: It took a lot of persuasion to get her to come with us.
persuasion.example_tw: 費了好一番口舌才說服她跟我們一起去。

persuasive.example: The lawyer gave a very persuasive argument to the jury.
persuasive.example_tw: 律師向陪審團提出了非常有說服力的論點。

adopt.definition: to approve a method and put it into practice
adopt.example: Each group adopted a different strategy to solve the problem.
adopt.example_tw: 每個小組都採用了不同的策略來解決問題。

policy.definition: a plan adopted by an organization
policy.example: The government adopted stricter policies on recycling.
policy.example_tw: 政府採取了更嚴格的資源回收政策。

ensure.definition: to make certain that something happens
ensure.example: A new law will ensure that every child gets proper care.
ensure.example_tw: 一項新法律將確保每個孩子都得到妥善的照顧。

establish.definition: to start or found a school or business
establish.example_tw: 哈佛大學成立於 1636 年。

establishment.definition: the act of starting something new
establishment.example: The establishment of a new trade link will help the economy.
establishment.example_tw: 建立新的貿易聯繫將有助於經濟發展。

honor.definition: special award or title; treat with respect
honor.example: Ang Lee has received many honors and awards for his movies.
honor.example_tw: 李安因其電影獲得了許多榮譽和獎項。

honorable.example: The soldier received an award for his honorable service to his country.
honorable.example_tw: 這位士兵因其對國家的光榮服役而獲獎。

weapon.definition: highly effective strategy or dangerous tool
weapon.example: Exercising is considered to be an effective weapon against stress.
weapon.example_tw: 運動被認為是對抗壓力的有效手段。

dare.definition: to be bold enough to try or do something
dare.example: No one dares to walk alone in this area at night because of its high crime rate.
dare.example_tw: 因為犯罪率高，晚上沒有人敢獨自走在這個區域。

entertain.definition: to amuse or give pleasure to people
entertain.example: The clown entertained the kids by dancing and doing magic tricks in front of them.
entertain.example_tw: 小丑在孩子們面前跳舞和變魔術來逗樂他們。

entertainment.definition: the action of providing or being provided with amusement or enjoyment
entertainment.example: What did you do for entertainment during the long flight?
entertainment.example_tw: 在漫長的飛行中你做了什麼消遣？

imaginative.definition: having the ability to think creatively
imaginative.example: J. K. Rowling is an imaginative writer who has written many interesting fantasy stories.
imaginative.example_tw: J. K. 羅琳是一位富有想像力的作家，寫了許多有趣的奇幻故事。

imagine.definition: to form a mental image or concept of
imagine.example: Many people can't imagine life without a cellphone.
imagine.example_tw: 許多人無法想像沒有手機的生活。

imagination.definition: the faculty or action of forming new ideas, or images or concepts of external objects not present to the senses
imagination.example: My little brother is a child with a great imagination, which is shown in his colorful pictures.
imagination.example_tw: 我的弟弟是一個有著豐富想像力的孩子，這從他色彩繽紛的畫作中可以看出。

imaginary.example: This superhero film is set in an imaginary world.
imaginary.example_tw: 這部超級英雄電影設定在一個想像的世界中。

consistently.definition: in the same way over a long period of time
consistently.example: Jessica consistently gets the highest scores in her class because she studies the hardest.
consistently.example_tw: Jessica 在班上始終獲得最高分，因為她最用功。

consistent.translation: (態度、行為、品質等)始終如一的
consistent.definition: acting or done in the same way over time
consistent.example: This fast-food chain's food quality is very consistent. Their burgers always look and taste good, no matter where you buy them.
consistent.example_tw: 這家速食連鎖店的食物品質非常一致。無論你在哪裡買，他們的漢堡看起來和吃起來總是很棒。

convey.definition: to express one's thoughts, emotions, or attitudes
convey.example: This short film conveys the message that real beauty comes from being yourself.
convey.example_tw: 這部短片傳達了一個訊息：真正的美麗來自於做自己。

parade.definition: a public march to celebrate special events or days, usually held with music, costumes, and decorations
parade.example: The band and dancers performed as part of a special parade held down the city's main street on New Year's Eve.
parade.example_tw: 樂團和舞者在除夕夜沿著城市主要街道舉行的特別遊行中表演。

request.definition: the act of asking for something in a formal or polite manner
request.example: Judy's boss turned down her request for a pay raise because she was often late for work.
request.example_tw: Judy 的老闆拒絕了她加薪的要求，因為她經常上班遲到。

extreme.definition: considered unacceptable or unreasonable by most people
extreme.example: Barry has rather extreme views on gun control. He believes that even the police should be forbidden to carry weapons.
extreme.example_tw: Barry 對槍枝管制有相當極端的看法。他認為甚至應該禁止警察攜帶武器。

achieve.definition: to successfully reach a goal, usually with much effort
achieve.example: In the end, Bailey achieved her dream of competing in the Olympics.
achieve.example_tw: 最後，Bailey 達成了她參加奧運的夢想。

achievement.definition: a thing done successfully, typically by effort, courage, or skill
achievement.example: Winning the NBA's Most Valuable Player Award was one of Stephen Curry's greatest achievements.
achievement.example_tw: 贏得 NBA 最有價值球員獎是 Stephen Curry 最大的成就之一。

passion.definition: a powerful feeling, such as love or hate, about something
passion.example: Stephen has a passion for basketball, and he has never missed an NBA game.
passion.example_tw: Stephen 熱愛籃球，他從未錯過任何一場 NBA 比賽。

passionate.definition: showing or caused by strong feelings or a strong belief
passionate.example: Morgan is passionate about acting. She dreams of becoming a world-famous actress.
passionate.example_tw: Morgan 對演戲充滿熱情。她夢想成為一位世界著名的女演員。

hardship.definition: a difficult situation which is usually caused by one's lack of money or support
hardship.example: During the war, people in this country faced a lot of hardships.
hardship.example_tw: 戰爭期間，這個國家的人民面臨了許多艱難。

poverty.definition: a state in which one is very poor
poverty.example: Vincent van Gogh remained unknown during his lifetime and was often forced to live in extreme poverty.
poverty.example_tw: 文森·梵谷在世時默默無名，經常被迫生活在極度貧窮之中。

rescue.definition: to help or save someone or something in danger or difficulty
rescue.example: The coast guard rescued all the passengers from the sinking ship.
rescue.example_tw: 海岸防衛隊從沉船中救出了所有乘客。

career.definition: a job or a series of jobs one does during his or her working life
career.example: Not many people know that the band's lead singer began his career as a drummer.
career.example_tw: 沒有多少人知道這個樂團的主唱是以鼓手開始他的職業生涯的。

weave.definition: to produce items like cloth, baskets, carpets, or nets by crossing threads or strips over and under each other
weave.example: Grandma's picnic basket is woven from thin strips of bamboo.
weave.example_tw: 奶奶的野餐籃是用細竹條編織而成的。

deliver.definition: to provide information or ideas for someone
deliver.example: The movie delivers an inspiring view of life by documenting an Olympic champion's road to success.
deliver.example_tw: 這部電影透過記錄一位奧運冠軍的成功之路，傳達了鼓舞人心的人生觀。

delivery.definition: the action of delivering letters, packages, or ordered goods
delivery.example: This website allows you to buy goods and then pay for them on delivery to your house.
delivery.example_tw: 這個網站允許你購買商品，然後在貨物送到你家時付款。

disadvantage.definition: a condition or situation that makes someone or something less successful
disadvantage.example: Though a late start in the race put the cyclist at a disadvantage, she soon caught up with the leading group.
disadvantage.example_tw: 雖然在比賽中起步較晚讓這位自行車手處於劣勢，但她很快就趕上了領先集團。

overcome.definition: to solve or rise above a problem along the way to success and happiness
overcome.example: The passionate photographer overcame many hardships throughout his career.
overcome.example_tw: 這位充滿熱情的攝影師在他的職業生涯中克服了許多艱難。

attitude.definition: the way you see and act toward someone or something
attitude.example: Some people take a positive attitude toward graffiti and think that it is art.
attitude.example_tw: 有些人對塗鴉抱持積極的態度，認為它是一種藝術。

challenge.definition: something difficult that tests one's ability
challenge.example: Fiona wants to prove to her parents that she is mature enough to take on any challenge.
challenge.example_tw: Fiona 想向她的父母證明她已經夠成熟，可以接受任何挑戰。

obstacle.definition: a problem, challenge, or disadvantage blocking one's progress
obstacle.example: A lack of English language skills may be one of the major obstacles to finding a job.
obstacle.example_tw: 缺乏英語能力可能是找工作的主要障礙之一。

fulfill.definition: to complete or achieve what was aimed at or hoped for
fulfill.example: With her talent, Elsa should be able to easily fulfill her dream of becoming a famous artist.
fulfill.example_tw: 憑藉她的才華，Elsa 應該能夠輕鬆實現成為著名藝術家的夢想。
